- Hub API exposes local-only endpoints for add queue item, inspect readiness, start item, generate Codex prompt, and complete item with evidence; endpoints return stable JSON; endpoints reuse existing M17 operators; tests cover success and gated failure paths; no Hub mutation UI is added in this task.

Acceptance criteria:
- Contract is documented and testable.
- Dry-run/no-execute behavior is explicit.
- No Codex process invocation is added.
- No automatic queue execution is added.
- No GitHub API, gh, issue, PR, workflow, or external mutation behavior is added.

- Completion requires review evidence.
- No unattended multi-item execution is introduced.

work_items.title: M79.1 Codex CLI Windows Runner Hardening
work_items.description: Harden the Windows Codex CLI workflow after M79 by documenting and/or fixing sandbox Git permission limits, stdin prompt handoff reliability, and Windows output decoding behavior.
work_items.notes:
Acceptance criteria:
- Document or implement a reliable prompt handoff pattern so Codex receives the full prompt body, not only the first line.
- Document or implement Windows output decoding hardening for Codex dispatch/stdout/stderr capture.
- Document current Codex sandbox Git permission behavior where implementation can be authored by Codex but commit/push may remain operator-owned.
- Preserve local-only, operator-gated workflow boundaries.
- No automatic queue completion.
- No automatic next-item execution.

work_items.title: M79.2 Single-Item Ready-to-Codex Automation
work_items.description: Add a local operator-controlled workflow that takes one manually-ready queue item, generates the prompt, sends it to Codex CLI, waits for completion, records evidence, validates, commits/pushes implementation changes, completes the queue item, and commits/pushes queue evidence without starting any next item.
work_items.notes:
Acceptance criteria:
- Operator can manually toggle exactly one queue item to ready.
- A local command can process exactly one ready item end-to-end through prompt generation, Codex CLI dispatch, validation, evidence capture, implementation commit/push, queue completion, and queue evidence commit/push.
- The workflow must not start the next item automatically.
- The workflow must refuse to run if zero ready items or multiple ready items are found unless an explicit item_id is provided.
- The workflow must preserve local-only operation and avoid GitHub API/gh mutation.
- The workflow must use the M79.1 hardened stdin prompt handoff and tolerant output/run-state handling.
- Failed Codex runs must leave the queue item reviewable and must not mark completion.
- Validation failures must block completion and produce review evidence.
- Commit/push should be attempted only after validation passes.
- If commit/push fails, the workflow must record the failure and leave clear operator recovery steps.
work_items.validation_summary: Codex implemented M79.2 single-item ready-to-Codex automation. Focused validation passed: 263 passed. Full required bundle passed: 480 passed. Smoke checks passed. git diff --check passed with LF-to-CRLF warnings only.
work_items.evidence_note: Operator reviewed Codex output, implementation diff, validation results, commit, and push result. Codex authored the implementation but sandbox Git permissions blocked commit creation, so operator committed and pushed validated implementation manually. Codex transcript showed token usage footer before final message: tokens used 221,534, which should be targeted by M79.3.

work_items.title: M79.3 Codex Run Token Usage Capture
work_items.description: Capture and store token usage metadata for each Codex CLI run so future routing, cost analysis, and LLM decision matrix work can compare effort and usage across runs.
work_items.notes:
Acceptance criteria:
- Codex run state stores token usage fields when available.
- Run state should include prompt_tokens, completion_tokens, total_tokens, model, provider, reasoning_effort, and source/extraction status where available.
- If token usage cannot be extracted, the run state must record token_usage_available=false and explain why.
- Token metadata must be exposed through inspect-codex-dispatch-run.
- Token metadata must be usable by future decision matrix/routing work.
- Tests must cover available token usage, missing token usage, malformed usage payloads, and backward compatibility with old run_state files.

work_items.title: M78.5 Operator Workflow Compression and Prompt Builder Agent Contract
work_items.description: Compress the current manual queue-to-prompt-to-Codex workflow by adding an operator workflow preparation layer and a Prompt Builder Agent contract that generates high-quality reviewed prompt artifacts before dispatch.
work_items.notes:
Acceptance criteria:
- Add a workflow preparation command that reduces manual inspection/start/prompt-generation steps.
- Add a Prompt Builder Agent / Prompt Architect Agent contract.
- Generated prompts must use queue item context, docs, routing metadata, model/engine target, safety gates, and validation requirements.
- Prompt Builder creates prompt artifacts only.
- Prompt Builder does not execute prompts, call Codex, invoke local LLMs, mutate source files, or advance queue items automatically.
- Workflow preparation must remain local-only and operator-gated.
- No automatic next-item execution.
- No unattended multi-item execution.
- No GitHub API, gh, issue, PR, workflow, or GitHub mutation.

No automatic dispatch, automatic queue completion, automatic next-item execution, GitHub API, gh, issue, PR, workflow mutation, or local LLM expansion was added.

work_items.title: M79.4 Codex Dispatch Recovery and Windows argv Hardening
work_items.description: Harden Codex dispatch recovery after partial failures and fix Windows command/argv handling discovered during M80 manual recovery.
work_items.notes:
Acceptance criteria:
- run-single-ready-codex-queue-item can recover or resume safely after a partial failure where the item is already in_progress.
- Recovery must not start a next queue item automatically.
- Existing approved dispatch runs can be continued or safely superseded by a new approved run with clear evidence.
- Windows command paths must be preserved correctly, including backslash paths and forward-slash paths.
- --command must not be dropped when --command-arg is present.
- Codex CLI dispatch should support codex.cmd exec non-interactive execution on Windows.
- Dispatch must block if the prompt artifact is empty before execution.
- Run state should clearly record command argv, resolved executable, prompt bytes, exit code, stderr, stdout, token usage availability, and recovery guidance.
- Tests must cover command path preservation, command plus args, empty prompt blocking, in_progress recovery, and failed dispatch recovery.
- Source-of-truth docs must document the recovered Windows Codex workflow.
- Preserve local-only, operator-gated boundaries.
- No automatic next-item execution.
- No GitHub API, gh, issue, PR, workflow, or external mutation behavior.
work_items.validation_summary: M79.4 Codex dispatch recovery and Windows argv hardening was implemented and committed. Focused validation passed with 177 tests. Required validation bundle passed with 493 tests. git diff --check passed with line-ending warnings only.
